channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US sanctions group that builds illegal West Bank settlements, with close ties to Israeli government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. on Monday imposed sanctions on organizations and firms involved in illegal settlement development in the occupied West Bank, including a well-established decades-old group that has close ties with Israeli leadership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court-martialed military veteran sentenced to over 4 years in prison for Capitol riot attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former U.S. Army soldier who was court-martialed for fatally shooting a handcuffed civilian in Iraq two decades ago was sentenced on Monday to more than four years in prison for his role in the attack on the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021. Edward Richmond Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Moscow warns the US over allowing Ukraine to hit Russian soil with longer-range weapons
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Kremlin warned Monday that President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russia with U.S.-supplied longer-range missiles adds “fuel to the fire” of the war and would escalate international tensions even higher.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Southwest employee welcomes Chiefs fans by playing Bills song over intercom
item.description: She said she wanted to let Chiefs fans know that "the Buffalo Bills have the best fans in the league.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: New York teen uses passion for bowling, engineering to inspire others
item.description: “As a little kid, seeing all these huge machines, just to set up bowling pins, it's like ‘Wow,’" said Joe Dayer, known as “Joe the Pro.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Former officer sentenced for beating a man with a baton 45 times in Los Angeles
item.description: A former police officer with the Veterans Affairs Police Department (VAPD) has been sentenced for hitting an unarmed man dozens of times with a baton, officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KREX 5

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday amid debate over Gaetz report
item.description: Members of the House Ethics Committee will meet Wednesday, a source confirmed to The Hill, as the panel weighs whether it should release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), whom President-elect Trump plans to nominate to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Families can cut down their own Christmas tree at Land Between the Lakes
item.description: "Taking the family out into the woods to look for the perfect Christmas tree is a great holiday tradition.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Texas lawmaker files bill to help Rio Grande Valley farmers affected by border water crisis
item.description: U.S. Rep. Monica De La Cruz, R-Texas, has filed legislation that, if approved, would give millions of dollars to farmers in the Rio Grande Valley affected by a lack of water payments from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Steve Bannon's trial in a border wall charity scheme case delayed until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Conservative strategist Steve Bannon won't face trial until February on state charges of conspiring to dupe donors to build a border wall, a judge said Monday as prosecutors asked for the jury to be anonymous.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump’s defense pick reignites the debate
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought was settled long ago: Should women be allowed to serve their country by fighting on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Top pollster Ann Selzer is set to retire from the Des Moines Register’s Iowa Poll after a shocking presidential survey was so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Testimony resumes in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Testimony resumed Monday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, a case that became a flashpoint in the national debate over immigration during this year's presidential campaign. Jose Ibarra, who entered the U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Camping murder suspect claimed dog led to tent killing: docs
item.description: A report revealed how the suspect in the murder of avid outdoorsman Dustin Kjersem made employees at a popular Montana resort feel "uncomfortable," documents show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Netflix users report outages during Tyson-Paul boxing match
item.description: Just as thousands of Netflix users flocked to the app to stream Friday night's head-to-head boxing match between champion Mike Tyson and YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul, the stream crashed.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI investigates offensive messages targeting Hispanic, LGBTQ people
item.description: The announcement comes days after African American students received similar messages stating they’d been selected to "pick cotton.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southwest flight evacuated after cell phone catches fire
item.description: Passengers of a Southwest Airlines flight sitting at a Denver International Airport gate had to evacuate after a cell phone battery caught on fire.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as White House press secretary
item.description: Leavitt, 27, previously served in the Trump White House as assistant press secretary under Kayleigh McEnany, now a leading host on Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of hospitality workers are on strike at a casino near the Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Hundreds of hospitality workers at a casino near the Las Vegas Strip walked off the job just before dawn Friday amid a long and highly contentious fight for a new contract.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trial begins for the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — A Venezuelan man "went hunting for females on the University of Georgia’s campus” earlier this year and ended up killing nursing student Laken Riley after a struggle, a prosecutor said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 of RFK Jr.'s most controversial views
item.description: President-elect Trump's decision to nominate Robert F. Kennedy Jr. for Health and Human Services (HHS) secretary will put some of the former independent presidential candidate's most controversial views under deep scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: What happens next in The Onion's effort to buy Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: The Onion’s winning bid for Alex Jones ’ Infowars platform is under review by a federal bankruptcy judge after Jones and his lawyers complained about how an auction was conducted.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida doctor accused of hitting, killing man in wheelchair, then driving off
item.description: A Florida doctor was taken into custody Wednesday after detectives said they were able to connect him to a deadly hit-and-run that happened in mid-October.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, Fox 5

item.title: Texas high court says execution in 'shaken baby syndrome' case can't be halted by lawmaker subpoena
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The Texas Supreme Court on Friday ruled that a legislative subpoena cannot stop an execution after Republican and Democratic lawmakers who say Robert Roberson is innocent used the novel maneuver to pause his execution at the last minute.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why you should throw away your black plastic cooking utensils
item.description: The tools you cook with may be introducing an unwanted ingredient into your family dinner: flame retardant.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: The world's tallest teenager, 7-foot-9 Olivier Rioux, plans to redshirt at Florida this season
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — The world’s tallest teenager might have to wait a year to become the world’s tallest college basketball player. Florida coach Todd Golden says Olivier Rioux, a 7-foot-9 freshman who owns a spot in the Guinness record book, is planning to redshirt.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Mike Tyson slapped Jake Paul at their final weigh-in. Here's why Tyson claims he did it
item.description: A meeting between Mike Tyson and Jake Paul briefly devolved into chaos on Thursday after Tyson slapped Paul across the face during the final weigh-in before their highly anticipated fight on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Judge sets April trial for 3 former officers charged with murder in Tyre Nichols' beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers charged with murder in the beating of Tyre Nichols will go on trial in April, a judge decided Friday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police: Trump cabinet pick Hegseth involved in 2017 sexual assault investigation
item.description: Hegseth, who Donald Trump recently tapped to lead the Department of Defense, was investigated for sexual assault in 2017, the Monterey Police Department said.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, Fox 5

item.title: Conan O’Brien will host 2025 Oscars
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A year after turning to comedian Jimmy Kimmel to host their big show, The Academy Awards will pivot to another familiar TV funnyman — Conan O’Brien. “America demanded it and now it’s happening: Taco Bell’s new Cheesy Chalupa Supreme.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside one of the most consequential UFO encounters of all time: the 'tic tac' incident
item.description: Pentagon officials did their best to downplay the incident, and the US Navy didn't bother to investigate, but the "Tic Tac" incident did lead to a significant investigation even if the public wasn't made aware until years later.
item.dc:creator: George Knapp, WGN 9
